calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-17-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-02-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues (C.C.P. 437c)
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Peter Henry Liederman of the law offices of Peter Henry Liederman (510.681.6916) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Robert J. Frassetto of the law offices of Frassetto Law LLP (510.817.0466) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

Court discloses that Judge Kahn was trial and sentencing judge in related criminal action. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO GIANTS ENTERPRISES LLC, CHINA BASIN BALLPARK COMPANY LLC, and SAN FRANCISCO BASEBALL ASSOCIATES LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues (C.C.P. 437c) is DENIED in its entirety. The moving defendants failed to maintain their initial summary judgment/adjudication burden of production. Ms. DeLaura's complaint alleges that defendants owed a duty to provide security and prevent the attack as well as a separate duty to provide adequate assistance to injured ballpark patrons. (Complaint, pars. 20 and 29.) Ms. DeLaura's complaint alleges that she was injured, defendants owed a duty to provide timely assistance, defendants failed to provide timely assistance and Ms. DeLaura was left bleeding for at least 13 minutes, and defendants' conduct caused harm. Defendants' motion only meaningfully addresses the first duty regarding security. While Defendants argue that the delay in failing to attend to Ms. DeLaura did not cause her harm, defendants present no evidence to support that assertion. Because defendants do not sufficiently address the distinct theory of liability based on defendants' alleged failure to attend to Ms. DeLaura after she was injured, the motion is denied. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.
